---
title: "Electronics: From Elements to Systems"
description: "A free, practical guide from first measurements to complete electronic systems."
page-role: frontmatter
---

Electronics becomes useful when its descriptions agree. The assembly, schematic,
equations, simulation, firmware, measured waveform, requirement, and acceptance
test all describe the same system from different angles. Agreement gives
confidence in the model. Disagreement tells you where to investigate.

This book develops that discipline from safe first measurements to complete
electronic systems. You will learn to choose an appropriate model, state its
limits, test its predictions, and make decisions that the available evidence
supports. The emphasis is practical: important ideas lead to calculations,
measurements, design choices, or diagnostic tests rather than ending with a
formula.

## The route

The seven parts follow the way an electronic system grows:

| Part | Focus |
|---|---|
| Electrical foundations | Safe practice, circuit quantities, networks, measurement, and dynamic behavior |
| Devices and analog circuits | Semiconductor devices, amplification, feedback, sensing, and analog limits |
| Digital hardware | Logic, state, timing, hardware description, memory, and FPGA systems |
| Embedded systems | Software at the hardware boundary, peripherals, concurrency, networks, and dependable operation |
| System domains | Signals, conversion, control, power, communication, and edge applications |
| Product realization | Requirements, PCB design, integrity, manufacture, test, safety, and lifecycle |
| Complete systems | Cross-domain projects that must be verified, documented, and handed over |

The mathematics and engineering-practice appendices provide working references
when a chapter first needs them. They are not entrance requirements.

The default reading order follows the sidebar. If you already have some of the
prerequisites or are working toward a particular application, use the
[reading roadmap](roadmap.qmd) to choose a route without skipping a dependency.

## How to begin

The [preface](preface.qmd) introduces the book's notation, evidence workflow,
and practical boundaries. [Safe bench practice](01-foundations/f01-safe-practice.qmd)
is the first chapter on the default route.

The book is free and remains in active development. Each chapter identifies its
current maturity, and the roadmap explains what that status means. No chapter
replaces a datasheet, an applicable standard, supervised laboratory training, or
local authorization for hazardous work.
